title: GameObject + Transform — World/Local Space, Parenting, Hierarchy
summary: Core semantics of Unity's Transform component: world vs local space, position/rotation/scale relationships across parent-child hierarchies, the TransformPoint/InverseTransformPoint API family, SetParent's worldPositionStays boolean, and common traps when constructing scenes programmatically. The most common bug class in code-generated scenes — especially for procedural simulations — is mixing world and local coordinates or setting position after reparenting without updating to localPosition. Read this before programmatically placing, parenting, or transforming GameObjects.

llmGuidance.when_placing_a_newly_created_object_at_a: Set transform.position AFTER SetParent, OR use transform.localPosition if you mean 'relative to parent'. Setting position BEFORE parenting with worldPositionStays=true is fine; Unity back-computes localPosition. The #1 bug: calling SetParent then position = zero and expecting parent origin — that moves to WORLD origin.
llmGuidance.when_converting_between_world_and_local_: Use transform.TransformPoint(localPoint) to go local→world (applies translation, rotation, scale). Use transform.InverseTransformPoint(worldPoint) for world→local. For vectors without translation use TransformVector/InverseTransformVector. For unit directions (forward, normals) use TransformDirection/InverseTransformDirection — ignores scale.
llmGuidance.when_setting_scale_on_a_parent_that_has_: transform.lossyScale is an APPROXIMATION. When a rotated parent is combined with non-uniform scale the true accumulated transform is a sheared 3x3 matrix, not a diagonal scale — lossyScale becomes inaccurate. Avoid non-uniform scale on rotated parents if colliders or lossyScale reads matter.
llmGuidance.when_rotating_programmatically: Store and manipulate rotations as Quaternions, not Euler angles. Build Quaternions via Quaternion.Euler(x,y,z), Quaternion.AngleAxis(angle,axis), or Quaternion.LookRotation(forward,up). Slerp for camera/character rotation, Lerp only for small deltas. Raw eulerAngles mutation causes drift because Quaternion→Euler is not unique.
llmGuidance.when_reparenting_without_changing_appear: SetParent(newParent, worldPositionStays: true) — Unity recomputes local values so world pose is preserved. Default overload (single arg) is equivalent. If you pass false, localPosition/rotation/scale are kept and world pose changes.
llmGuidance.when_finding_components_in_a_hierarchy: GetComponentInChildren<T>() and GetComponentInParent<T>() include self. Use the includeInactive overload (default false) to also search disabled GameObjects. For direct children only, iterate transform.GetChild(i).

position.relationship: position = parent.TransformPoint(localPosition); equal when no parent.
position.commonUse: position for spawn points and physics. localPosition for offsets within rigs / prefabs.

rotation.relationship: rotation == parent.rotation * localRotation when parent exists
rotation.commonUse: Always store as Quaternion. Convert to/from Euler only for UI.

scale.worldApi: transform.lossyScale (Vector3, read-only, lossy under rotated parents)
scale.relationship: lossyScale ≈ parent.lossyScale × localScale, but inaccurate when ancestor rotation combines with non-uniform scale — produces shear not representable as a diagonal Vector3.
scale.commonUse: Set localScale. Read lossyScale cautiously; check parents are uniformly scaled first.

euler.pitfall: Reading after setting may return a different-but-equivalent triple. Gimbal lock near ±90° pitch. Store rotations as Quaternions; convert to Euler only for UI display.

parentingBehavior.trueBehavior: World pose preserved. localPosition/Rotation/Scale are recomputed.
parentingBehavior.falseBehavior: Local values preserved. World pose changes.
parentingBehavior.noteRectTransform: RectTransform (UI) inherits Transform but adds anchoredPosition, anchorMin/Max, pivot, sizeDelta. UI layout math overrides naive localPosition reasoning.

hierarchyApis.behavior: Searches immediate children by name; supports slash-paths 'A/B/C' for descendants.
Direct child by index. Pair with childCount.
Depth-first, includes self. Inactive GOs skipped by default.
Walks up the hierarchy including self. Inactive GOs skipped by default.
Reorder among siblings. Affects UI draw order.

decisionRules.scenario: Programmatically creating a GameObject and placing it relative to an existing object
decisionRules.rule: 1) Create or find parent. 2) Create child, then SetParent(parent, true). 3) Set child.localPosition = offsetFromParent. DO NOT set position=zero and expect parent origin — that goes to world origin.
decisionRules.evidence: Fluid session 2026-04-20: fluid manager repeatedly had particles simulated in local space but emitted in world space due to mixing transform.position with local spawn intent.

decisionRules.scenario: Converting a mouse/screen world point to a coordinate inside a rotated prefab
decisionRules.rule: parent.InverseTransformPoint(worldPoint). Don't subtract parent.position — that ignores rotation/scale.

decisionRules.scenario: Need to scale a group uniformly
decisionRules.rule: Set localScale on the parent. Avoid non-uniform scale on rotated parents — causes shear, breaks lossyScale, and warns at colliders.
decisionRules.evidence: Unity physics manual on non-uniform scale.

decisionRules.scenario: Saving/restoring a world pose across reparent operations
decisionRules.rule: SetParent(newParent, worldPositionStays: true) — preserves world pose. Default behavior; the single-arg overload is equivalent.

commonPitfalls.symptom: After SetParent, setting transform.position = Vector3.zero moves object to world origin instead of parent origin.
commonPitfalls.cause: position is world-space. Using it post-reparent assumes world coords.
commonPitfalls.fix: Use transform.localPosition = Vector3.zero if you mean parent origin.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Instantiated prefab appears at unexpected location when passed a parent.
commonPitfalls.cause: Instantiate(prefab, parent) preserves the prefab's world pose; to drop it at parent origin you need Instantiate(prefab, parent, worldPositionStays: false) or explicit localPosition reset after.
commonPitfalls.fix: Use the 3-arg Instantiate overload or set localPosition after.

commonPitfalls.symptom: lossyScale reads wrong on a child of a rotated parent with non-uniform scale.
commonPitfalls.cause: Combined rotation + non-uniform scale produces shear; lossyScale flattens to Vector3 and loses information.
commonPitfalls.fix: Only read lossyScale when ancestors are uniformly scaled, or use localToWorldMatrix decomposition for accurate results.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Accumulating small eulerAngles writes cause rotation drift.
commonPitfalls.cause: Quaternion→Euler is not unique; reconversion yields different triples.
commonPitfalls.fix: Store rotation as Quaternion; apply deltas with Quaternion.Euler(delta) * currentRotation.

commonPitfalls.symptom: GetComponentInChildren returns null even though component exists.
commonPitfalls.cause: Component is on a disabled GameObject; default includeInactive=false skips it.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Colliders misbehave under sheared / non-uniform parent scale.
commonPitfalls.cause: Unity colliders assume rigid transforms; shear breaks collision math.
commonPitfalls.fix: Flatten scale into the mesh/collider at authoring time; or use uniform scale on colliders' GameObjects.

build: Quaternion.Euler(x, y, z) — from designer-friendly angles
Quaternion.AngleAxis(angle, axis) — rotation around a known axis
Quaternion.LookRotation(forward, up) — orient toward a target
interpolate: Quaternion.Slerp — constant angular velocity; use for cameras and characters
Quaternion.Lerp — cheaper, non-uniform; only for very small deltas
avoid: Mutating eulerAngles components — struct, doesn't compile
Reading/writing eulerAngles in a loop — drift and gimbal artifacts

versionDeltas.change: Transform APIs are extremely stable. All methods covered here (TransformPoint, SetParent, localPosition, etc.) predate 2021.3 by many versions.

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from publicly-documented Unity APIs and Manual pages. API signatures, method behaviors, and mathematical relationships (position = parent.TransformPoint(localPosition)) are factual. Explanatory prose, decision rules, llmGuidance, and commonPitfalls are original Mosaic editorial content. Unity Manual and ScriptReference pages are cited as externalReferences and NOT reproduced here.
